questions.question: What does the Single Responsibility Principle state?
options: A class should have only one method
A class should have only one reason to change
A class should only be used by one other class
A class should implement only one interface
questions.explanation: SRP means a class should have only one reason to change — one responsibility. If a class handles both persistence and email sending, it has two reasons to change.

questions.question: What does 'composition over inheritance' mean?
options: Never use inheritance in Java
Prefer holding references to objects (has-a) over extending classes (is-a) for code reuse
Compose methods from smaller methods
Use multiple inheritance via interfaces
questions.explanation: Composition over inheritance means preferring 'has-a' relationships (holding object references) over 'is-a' (extends). This reduces coupling and increases flexibility.

options: Single Responsibility
Open/Closed
Interface Segregation
Dependency Inversion
questions.explanation: Interface Segregation Principle: clients shouldn't depend on methods they don't use. A Developer doesn't need serveCoffee(). Split into smaller focused interfaces.

questions.question: What does the Law of Demeter discourage?
options: Using inheritance
Chaining method calls on returned objects (train wrecks like a.getB().getC().doX())
Having too many classes
Using static methods
questions.explanation: The Law of Demeter (principle of least knowledge) says don't reach through objects to access their internals. Method chains like a.getB().getC() create tight coupling.

questions.question: High cohesion means:
options: A class depends on many other classes
A class has many public methods
All methods and fields in a class relate to a single, focused purpose
A class is inherited by many subclasses
questions.explanation: High cohesion means a class's methods and fields are closely related and focused on a single responsibility. Low cohesion means a class does many unrelated things.
